title: T-012 — API: PO state transition via rule registry (submit / cancel)
prompt:
Implement Server Actions `submitPurchaseOrder(po_id)` and `cancelPurchaseOrder(po_id, reason)` that delegate to the `po_state_machine_v1` rule from T-007. Submit transitions draft→submitted (or submitted→pending_approval if planning_settings.po_require_approval && total > threshold). Cancel allowed only in {draft, submitted, pending_approval}. Each transition writes an outbox event with payload from §6.5 example.

RED test exercises happy path, V-PLAN-PO-003 lines>=1 guard failure, and forbidden cancel from confirmed (returns 409).

pipeline_inputs.description: PO submit/cancel Server Actions backed by rule registry transitions.
pipeline_inputs.details: Both actions call `workflowEngine.transition({ entity: 'po', id, action })`. Engine resolves `po_state_machine_v1`, evaluates guards, fires side-effects. Cancel reason stored in po.notes audit + outbox payload. For confirmed→cancelled an admin override flow returns a structured 409 with `{ code: 'PO_CONFIRMED_CANCEL_REQUIRES_OVERRIDE', overrideEndpoint: '...' }`.
acceptance_criteria: Given a draft PO with 1 line, when submitPurchaseOrder runs, then status becomes 'submitted' and outbox event 'po.state_changed' is inserted in same transaction.
Given a draft PO with 0 lines, when submitPurchaseOrder runs, then guard V-PLAN-PO-003 returns a 400 with code 'V-PLAN-PO-003' and PO remains 'draft'.
Given a PO in 'confirmed' state, when cancelPurchaseOrder runs, then HTTP 409 with code 'PO_CONFIRMED_CANCEL_REQUIRES_OVERRIDE' is returned.
Given `pnpm vitest run src/server/actions/planning/__tests__/po-transitions.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for happy submit, V-PLAN-PO-003 guard, confirmed-cancel block, outbox emit.
risk_red_lines: Do not write transition logic outside the rule engine — must call workflowEngine.transition.
Do not skip outbox event — analytics depend on it.
Do not allow cancel of confirmed PO without explicit admin-override audit entry.
